name: Randy's Maple Walnut Ice Cream
recipeInstructions:
1) Preheat the oven to 250 degrees Fahrenheit and toast the walnut pieces until they are lightly browned. Allow them to cool completely.
2) Heat the milk with half a cup of chopped walnuts in a heavy saucepan over medium heat. When the mixture is close to boiling but not boiling, remove from the heat; cover and let steep for 20 minutes. Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the walnut pieces.
3) Whisk together the egg yolks and maple sugar in a separate bowl. Gradually add the warm milk while whisking constantly to ensure no eggs are cooked. Return this mixture to the saucepan and cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until it coats the back of a spoon (about 170-175 degrees Fahrenheit).
4) Pour the heavy cream into a large bowl or container. Strain the custard through a fine-mesh sieve into the cream. Mix well, then chill thoroughly in the refrigerator.
5) Freeze according to the manufacturer's instructions for your ice cream maker. Once the ice cream has thickened (about 1-2 minutes before it is fully frozen), fold in the remaining chopped walnuts.
This recipe makes approximately 1 quart.
